objective: Find no-cache parser/scanner bottlenecks and low-risk optimizations for parseAspDocument <=23.25ms.
result_json: {"packet_id":"P1","findings":["Read-only no-generator measurement against existing samples/dist: 55 files / 3.55 MB, parseAspDocumentAsync with clearAspCoreCaches before/after each source had median 30.08 ms over 5 measured runs, above the <=23.25 ms target. This is not the official pnpm benchmark because scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs always runs the sample generator first.","The no-cache benchmark clears core and embedded caches around every source (scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs:131-145), so async parse-cache/content-cache changes are cache-only and should be rejected for this target. Improvements need to reduce cold skeleton parse work.","parseAspDocumentAsync currently goes through parseAspDocumentSkeletonTypeScript; its hot work is scanHtmlAndAsp, then directive extraction, script-region language normalization, buildRegions, node construction/sort, directive node lookup, and nodeToRegion roundtrip (packages/core/src/parser.ts:170-255). Most of that allocation/sort work repeats on every uncached source.","Directive parsing is duplicated: scanHtmlAndAsp parses directive content to update scanner scriptLanguage (asp-scanner.ts:63-65,190-202), and parser.ts parses the same directive regions again for AspDirective objects/defaultLanguage (parser.ts:178-199). This repeats slice/trim/split/parseAttributes/rangeFromOffsets in the no-cache parse path.","buildRegions sorts embedded regions, then sorts nested regions, then parseAspDocumentSkeletonTypeScript sorts nodes again and maps nodes back into regions (parser.ts:214-232,1135-1191). For the huge sample shape observed here (11195 ASP regions, 2770 script tags, 2770 style tags), the extra array spreads/sorts/object recreation are plausible no-cache bottlenecks.","scanHtmlAndAsp/readHtmlTag scans attributes for every non-closing tag to decide whether to parse attribute spans, using containsAsciiCaseInsensitive(..., 'style') (asp-scanner.ts:246-254,614-637). The current huge sample has zero style attributes, so this is mostly false-positive/negative scanning work; script/style/object tags still need full attributes, but ordinary tags should be fast-rejected more cheaply.","readHtmlTag allocates for selfClosing with text.slice(...).trimEnd().endsWith('/') whenever attributes were parsed (asp-scanner.ts:269-270). This is small but repeated for script/style/object/style-attribute candidates and can be replaced by a backwards char-code scan without changing behavior.","include comment parsing slices and lowercases comment bodies before rejecting non-include comments (asp-scanner.ts:541-549). The huge sample has 3110 HTML comments but only 54 include directives, so an in-place first-non-whitespace/#include check would reduce scanner allocations on no-cache runs."],"recommendations":["First target parser/scanner allocation reduction, not caches. Add a focused parseAspDocument benchmark gate before and after each patch with ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=parseAspDocument and ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1.","Have scanHtmlAndAsp return parsed directives and/or scanner-derived defaultLanguage, since it is only used by parser.ts. Reuse that data in both skeleton and full parse paths to remove duplicate directive parsing. Risk is low if parser directive tests cover LANGUAGE/defaultLanguage and directive attributes.","Avoid the skeleton node-to-region roundtrip. Preserve directive attributes by carrying a directivesByOffset map into region/node construction or by annotating directive regions once, then return the built regions directly instead of nodes.map(nodeToRegion). This removes one pass and many short-lived objects.","Reduce sorting/array churn in buildRegions + node construction: avoid spread-created combined arrays where practical, skip nestedRegions sort when no nested regions were accepted, and avoid sorting nodes when regions are already in source order and includes can be merged by offset. Keep compareRegionsBySourceRange semantics for nested inline ASP inside script/style.","Replace containsAsciiCaseInsensitive(..., 'style') with a cheap attribute-name scanner such as hasStyleAttributeName(text,start,end,scriptLanguage), and skip it entirely when attributesStart >= attributesEnd. It should recognize a real style attribute name, ignore ASP islands as parseAttributeSpans already does, and avoid parsing all attributes for tags where only a substring like data-style appears.","Replace selfClosing detection slice/trimEnd with a backward char-code scan over the attribute range.","Optimize parseIncludeComment with in-place checks: advance leading whitespace by index, verify #include case-insensitively before allocating commentBody/body strings, then slice only the attribute text for actual include comments.","Keep full sync parse behavior covered separately: full parse still builds region tokens and VB CST (parser.ts:878-915,1066-1129), while the benchmark operation uses the async skeleton path. Do not regress parseAspDocument/parseAspCst callers while optimizing parseAspDocumentAsync."],"risk":"Low-to-medium. The safest wins are allocation/pass reductions in parser.ts and char-code scanner fast paths. The main semantic risks are Classic ASP directive LANGUAGE handling, ASP islands inside tag attributes, include range preservation, and script/style/server-script wrapper ranges; add targeted parser/scanner regression tests before relying on benchmark movement.","suggested_commands":["pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core exec vitest run test/parser.test.ts","pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core run typecheck","ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=parseAspDocument ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=7 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=1 pnpm run benchmark:huge","ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=7 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=1 pnpm run benchmark:huge","ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=7 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=1 pnpm run benchmark:huge"]}

objective: Find no-cache virtual document bottlenecks and optimizations for build/html/css/javascript target medians.
result_json: {"packet_id":"P2","findings":["Read-only inspection only; no edits, staging, commits, or pushes. Existing huge sample stats from packages/core/dist parsing: 55 files, 3.55MB, 44,550 regions total, with 27,815 html, 2,770 css, 2,770 javascript, and 11,195 ASP regions; default.asp alone has 8,870 regions. That shape makes per-region scans and full-source masking visible in no-cache medians.","scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs clears both core and embedded benchmark caches before and after every source when ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 (lines 131-145), and scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs does the same through clearAllBenchmarkCaches (lines 666-680). Cache-only ideas are therefore rejected for these target medians unless they reduce work inside a single source operation before the clear.","packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts buildVirtualDocuments already groups regions once and shares sortedRegions for the all-language build (lines 19-43), but it still builds html, css, javascript, and jscript eagerly. In a local read-only timing over existing huge samples with caches cleared around each source, buildVirtualDocuments-only was dominated by virtual construction at roughly 48.7ms median, while cssVirtualDocument-only and javascriptVirtualDocument-only were roughly 3ms each; htmlVirtualDocument-only was roughly 34.4ms. That points to HTML masking and source-map setup as the main virtual-doc bottleneck, not CSS/JS concatenation alone.","The HTML path buildMaskedDocument preserves full source length by walking every non-HTML gap with preserveLineEndingsRange and materializing spaces/newlines (lines 357-387 and 337-355). For huge files this is a full-text allocation/scanning cost even when the caller only needs the virtual text length or later scans HTML diagnostics.","The CSS/JS path repeatedly allocates nestedRegions and then filters holes separately: nestedRegionsForOwner builds an array (lines 204-218), maskNestedRegions walks it (lines 234-255), and sourceMapSegmentsForRegion filters it again (lines 149-167). This is uncached per source and per language, so combining these passes can reduce no-cache buildVirtualDocuments and css/javascript virtual medians without relying on cache hits.","virtualDocumentFromBody always creates a SourceMap immediately (lines 390-401), and createSourceMap eagerly calls sourceSegmentsForLookup (lines 472+ in the same file). Many benchmark operations only read virtual.text.length, and diagnostics mostly use virtual-to-source mapping. Eager source-side lookup preparation is avoidable no-cache work if toVirtualOffset/toVirtualPosition lazily prepare sourceSegments only when needed.","scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs buildEmbeddedVirtualDocument filters parsed.regions for each language operation before calling core.buildVirtualDocument (lines 174-193). This is smaller than HTML masking but still O(region count) uncached work per operation; it also means html/css/javascript virtual medians include parser plus script-side filter overhead before core construction.","The current local quick timing with parse included was roughly parseAspDocument 35.2ms, buildVirtualDocuments 56.1ms, htmlVirtualDocument 46.5ms, cssVirtualDocument 27.3ms, javascriptVirtualDocument 27.4ms. Exact numbers are environment-sensitive and not an official benchmark run, but they show CSS/JS virtual targets likely need parse-side improvement too, while HTML needs virtual-doc work directly."],"recommendations":["Prioritize a lazy SourceMap source lookup change: keep segments for toSourceOffset, but defer sourceSegmentsForLookup sorting/checking until toVirtualOffset/toVirtualPosition is actually called. This directly cuts uncached virtual-document construction and should preserve API shape if sourceMap methods remain equivalent under tests.","Optimize buildMaskedDocument next. Consider a specialized line-ending mask builder for the HTML full-source document that avoids many small gap scans/strings, or precomputes newline runs once per document and emits masked gaps from that structure. Validate UTF-16 offsets and exact sourceMap ranges because HTML virtual text must remain source-length aligned.","For CSS/JS, merge nestedRegionsForOwner, maskNestedRegions, and sourceMapSegmentsForRegion into one owner-region pass that emits content chunks and source-map segments together. Avoid allocating nestedRegions and holes arrays per owner. Keep the current conservative ASP-hole masking semantics, especially CSS expression placeholders and JavaScript value placeholders.","Keep buildVirtualDocuments eager only if callers need all open virtuals. For benchmark and feature paths that request one language, add or use a parsed-aware single-language helper that can reuse per-parsed region metadata without building unrelated languages. Do not replace this with a cross-source body cache, because no-cache benchmark clears it.","In scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs, reduce measurement-only overhead after product changes: carry per-language region groups/fingerprint from parsed metadata when possible, or call a core helper instead of filtering parsed.regions in the harness. Treat this as secondary, because product code should get the main optimization first.","Do not spend time on virtualDocumentBodyCache size/key changes for this packet. The no-cache harness clears core caches around every source, so those changes will not move the requested medians unless they also reduce first-build work."],"risk":"Main correctness risk is source-map and UTF-16 offset drift: HTML virtual text must preserve source-length alignment, CSS/JS nested ASP masking must keep line endings and placeholder behavior, and lazy source-map setup must not change toSourceOffset/toVirtualOffset results. Benchmark-only script optimizations can hide product bottlenecks, so verify with both benchmark-huge.mjs and embedded-language-benchmark.mjs after product changes.","suggested_commands":["ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=7 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=1 pnpm run benchmark:huge","ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold ASP_LSP_BENCH_SAMPLE=huge ASP_LSP_BENCH_CONCURRENCY=1 pnpm exec node scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs","ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=buildVirtualDocuments node --cpu-prof scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs","pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core run test -- packages/core/test/parser.test.ts","pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run lint && pnpm run format:check"]}

objective: Find no-cache VBScript collect/analyze bottlenecks and low-risk optimizations for <50ms medians.
result_json: {"packet_id":"P3","findings":["Read-only inspection only; did not edit/stage/commit/push. Current worktree has unrelated untracked .workflow/asp-lsp-no-cache-benchmark-targets/.","No-cache collect/analyze benchmark paths call collectVbscriptSymbolsFromTextAsync/analyzeVbscriptFromTextAsync, and both currently call sync parseAspDocument before VB work (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:1803-1817,3048-3063; scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs:70-83). This means each no-cache VB operation pays full ASP parse plus VB CST creation, not the cheaper skeleton parse used by parseAspDocumentAsync (packages/core/src/parser.ts:45-67).","collectVbscriptSymbolsTypeScript always does implicit assignment discovery, type annotation application, assigned-type inference, and variant fallback (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:3070-3089). For huge uncached samples, the likely collect hot path is not caching but repeated statement/symbol visibility work.","addImplicitAssignmentSymbols scans every statement, then checks all current symbols with symbols.some plus isSymbolVisibleAt for each assignment target (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:3658-3722). On files with many assignment statements and many declared/implicit symbols, this becomes O(statements * symbols) and also misses the existing symbolIndexFor fast byLowerName path because it directly scans the whole array.","inferStatementTypes repeats statement.findIndex, visibleSymbolsByName, expression slice allocation, and expression inference for every assignment-like statement (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:3788-3823). This is uncached per operation and likely dominates collect after implicit assignment cleanup.","snapshotFor already centralizes documents/nodes/tokens/statements/declaration token maps, but it builds several intermediate arrays with flatMap/filter and recursive flattenVbNodes (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:5346-5401). This is internal-only and safe to optimize for no-cache runs because WeakMap caching does not help after benchmark cache clearing.","VB CST construction repeatedly computes statementEndIndex and statementTokens slices for the same statement while parsing (packages/core/src/vbscript-cst.ts:156-308,611-620). Set/CreateObject/ForEach/With paths call statementEndIndex multiple times and sometimes slice the remaining token tail, adding avoidable uncached parse cost.","analyzeVbscript layers diagnostics over symbol collection (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:1823-1860). Its no-cache risk areas are callSyntax over every statement and unusedSymbols usage indexing (packages/core/src/vbscript.ts:5843-6022,6106-6174), but unusedSymbols already has a candidate-name gate, so optimize collect first before changing diagnostics behavior."],"recommendations":["Highest priority: change FromText VB benchmark/API internals to parse via parseAspDocumentSkeletonAsync or parseAspDocumentSkeletonTypeScript plus hydrateVbscriptCst, then compare symbol/diagnostic parity against parseAspDocument. This targets no-cache work directly and avoids cache-only wins; keep public result shapes identical.","Replace addImplicitAssignmentSymbols' symbols.some full scan with a local symbolsByLowerName map initialized from current symbols and updated when implicit symbols are pushed. Use only candidates for lowerTarget and reuse a local scope-offset cache/index for isSymbolVisibleAt. Do not use the global WeakMap symbolIndexFor on a symbols array that is mutated unless the index is updated or rebuilt after mutation.","Use snapshotFor(parsed).serverScriptText for hasOptionExplicit or compute Option Explicit from existing token/doc data, avoiding a separate serverRegions slice/join before snapshotFor later rebuilds the same server script text in collect/analyze.","Make vbscript-cst statement parsing carry a precomputed endIndex/endOffset/tokens for the current statement. Pass that into createStatementNode/createDeclarationNode/SetNew/CreateObject/ForEach/With instead of recomputing statementEndIndex and slicing repeatedly.","Refactor snapshotFor to fill nodes, significantTokens, identifierTokens, prev/next maps, and statements in iterative single-pass loops rather than recursive flatten plus flatMap/filter chains. This is low-risk if tests assert same token/symbol behavior.","After the collect changes, use ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS=1 on analyzeVbscript to decide whether callSyntax or unusedSymbols still needs work. If callSyntax remains hot, add a precomputed callable-name Set/Map from symbols and skip visibleSymbolsByName for statements whose candidate name is not callable.","Reject cache-only changes such as increasing vbFromTextCache/asyncParseCache sizes or avoiding clearAspCoreCaches in benchmarks; the target explicitly clears caches between sources."],"risk":"Moderate overall. The best optimizations are internal and preserve API/result shape, but VBScript symbol visibility, implicit assignment behavior, and skeleton-vs-full parse parity are semantically sensitive. Validate with parser.test.ts plus direct symbol/diagnostic parity checks before trusting benchmark gains.","suggested_commands":["ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=collectVbscriptSymbols ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=5 pnpm run benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache","ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=analyzeVbscript ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=5 pnpm run benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache","pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core exec vitest run test/parser.test.ts --testNamePattern \"VBScript|implicit|diagnostic|skeleton|async\"","pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core run typecheck","pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core run test"]}

objective: Design final no-cache benchmark/test command matrix and identify measurement risks.
result_json: {"packet_id":"P4","findings":["package.json already exposes the needed huge benchmark modes: benchmark:huge:no-cache, benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache, benchmark:huge:hot:no-cache. Default cache mode is hot unless ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold is set.","scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs defaults to 1 warmup and 5 measured iterations, regenerates the huge sample before timing, builds core via the package script, and prints min/median/mean/max for each operation.","ASP_LSP_BENCH_DISABLE_CACHES=1 clears core and embedded benchmark caches before and after every source action, so cache-only wins should not count unless they also lower no-cache medians.","buildVirtualDocuments is measured as parseAspDocumentAsync plus buildVirtualDocuments for every source; htmlVirtualDocument/cssVirtualDocument/javascriptVirtualDocument are measured through runEmbeddedOperation, which also parses first, then builds the requested virtual document.","ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION can isolate each target operation without changing the harness, which is useful for failure triage after a full no-cache run.","Prior workflow notes say huge/large embedded harness alignment and non-zero CSS/JS fixture coverage matter before trusting embedded-language timings."],"recommendations":["Use benchmark:huge:no-cache as the primary package-script acceptance run because it matches the current no-cache script name, then run benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache as the guard against hidden URI-keyed cache dependence.","Treat the target medians as harness medians, not pure function medians: parseAspDocument <=23.25ms, buildVirtualDocuments <=32.04ms, collectVbscriptSymbols <50ms, analyzeVbscript <50ms, htmlVirtualDocument <=28.22ms, cssVirtualDocument <=23.64ms, javascriptVirtualDocument <=23.72ms.","Use at least 9 measured iterations and 2 warmups for final acceptance; default 5 iterations is acceptable for quick triage but too thin near thresholds.","If the full run fails, rerun only failing operations with ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION before changing code; prefer uncached parser/CST/virtual-document work over cache retention or hot-URI memoization ideas.","Do not enable ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS for acceptance because it adds timing overhead; use it only for analyzeVbscript diagnosis after confirming a failure.","Record both hot:no-cache and cold:no-cache outputs and report all repeated-run medians rather than selecting the fastest run."],"risk":"Main measurement risks are noise from only five default samples, hidden interpretation drift because several operations include parse cost, first-use/JIT effects that warmups do not cache away, accidental reliance on hot URI identity if only hot:no-cache is used, stale packages/core/dist if the build step is bypassed, and misleading analyzeVbscript numbers when debug-step instrumentation is enabled.","suggested_commands":["ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=9 pnpm run benchmark:huge:no-cache","ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=9 pnpm run benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache","for op in parseAspDocument buildVirtualDocuments collectVbscriptSymbols analyzeVbscript htmlVirtualDocument cssVirtualDocument javascriptVirtualDocument; do ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=$op ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=9 pnpm run benchmark:huge:no-cache; done","for op in parseAspDocument buildVirtualDocuments collectVbscriptSymbols analyzeVbscript htmlVirtualDocument cssVirtualDocument javascriptVirtualDocument; do ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=$op ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=9 pnpm run benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache; done","ASP_LSP_BENCH_OPERATION=analyzeVbscript ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=3 pnpm run benchmark:huge:cold:no-cache"]}
